description: Split MSSQL DDL on GO batch separators before ADO.NET execution. The emitted MSSQL DDL contains GO batch separators (from CoreDdlEmitter and RelationalModelDdlEmitter for triggers and views). GO is not valid T-SQL and will cause a syntax error when sent as a single SqlCommand. Acceptance: MssqlDatabaseProvisioner.ExecuteInTransaction() splits the SQL string on standalone GO lines (e.g., Regex.Split with pattern ^\s*GO\s*$ multiline+ignorecase), executes each non-empty batch as a separate SqlCommand within the same SqlTransaction, and ddl provision -d mssql succeeds on a schema with triggers and views.
steps-to-verify: Manually test: dms-schema ddl provision -s <schema> -c <mssql-connstr> -d mssql --create-database against a local SQL Server Docker container and verify it completes without GO syntax errors

description: Prevent MVCC ALTER DATABASE failure due to connection pooling. After ExecuteInTransaction disposes its SqlConnection to the target database, the pooled connection may block ALTER DATABASE SET READ_COMMITTED_SNAPSHOT ON from acquiring exclusive access. Acceptance: Either clear the connection pool after ExecuteInTransaction (SqlConnection.ClearPool), or append WITH ROLLBACK IMMEDIATE to the ALTER DATABASE statements in CheckOrConfigureMvcc, so that ddl provision -d mssql --create-database reliably configures MVCC without exclusive-access errors.
steps-to-verify: Review MssqlDatabaseProvisioner.ExecuteInTransaction() for pool clearing or MssqlDatabaseProvisioner.CheckOrConfigureMvcc() for WITH ROLLBACK IMMEDIATE
Manually test: dms-schema ddl provision -s <schema> -c <mssql-connstr> -d mssql --create-database and verify MVCC configuration succeeds without exclusive-lock errors

description: Make RCSI check in CheckOrConfigureMvcc defensive against driver return type. The pattern 'result is bool rcsiEnabled && !rcsiEnabled' may silently fail if ExecuteScalar returns a boxed int (0/1) instead of bool in some driver versions. Acceptance: Use Convert.ToBoolean(result) or handle both bool and int return types so the RCSI-off warning is always emitted when READ_COMMITTED_SNAPSHOT is disabled on an existing database.

Remove unnecessary CloneEffectiveSchemaSet call in DdlProvisionCommand. The provision command processes a single dialect so the schema set is never reused; the clone (copied from DdlEmitCommand's multi-dialect loop) is unnecessary. Acceptance: DdlProvisionCommand passes effectiveSchemaSet directly to the model builder without cloning, and the misleading deep-clone comment is removed or replaced.
steps-to-verify: Verify DdlProvisionCommand.cs no longer calls DdlCommandHelpers.CloneEffectiveSchemaSet

description: Fix misleading comment about Npgsql transaction opt-in in PgsqlDatabaseProvisioner.CreateDatabaseIfNotExists(). The comment at line 61-63 says 'Npgsql requires an explicit opt-in to run outside a transaction block' but no opt-in is performed and none is needed (Npgsql 7+ uses autocommit by default without explicit BeginTransaction). Acceptance: Comment is updated to: 'CREATE DATABASE cannot run inside a transaction in PostgreSQL. Without an explicit BeginTransaction(), Npgsql executes in autocommit mode.'
steps-to-verify: Review PgsqlDatabaseProvisioner.cs comment in CreateDatabaseIfNotExists for accuracy

description: Deduplicate connection-string parsing in MssqlDatabaseProvisioner and PgsqlDatabaseProvisioner. Both provisioners parse the connection string and extract the database name with identical null-check + InvalidOperationException logic in multiple methods (GetDatabaseName, CreateDatabaseIfNotExists, CheckOrConfigureMvcc). Acceptance: CreateDatabaseIfNotExists and CheckOrConfigureMvcc call GetDatabaseName() for extraction rather than re-parsing, eliminating the duplicated validation. Error messages remain consistent across all code paths.
steps-to-verify: Verify MssqlDatabaseProvisioner has a single database-name extraction path via GetDatabaseName()
Verify PgsqlDatabaseProvisioner has a single database-name extraction path via GetDatabaseName()
